title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. filifolium
intro: Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. filifolium, also known as yellow buttons, is a perennial plant native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. filifolium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and infections.
